studyPath.title: Recommended Study Path
studyPath.description: Three phases based on concept dependency — each phase builds on the one before it

phases.label: Foundation — Concepts and Infrastructure
phases.rationale: You cannot understand aseptic technique or BUD compliance without first understanding what a cleanroom is, why documentation exists, and how cleaning hierarchies work. These three modules establish the physical and regulatory foundation everything else builds on.

phases.label: Core Practice — Technique and Monitoring
phases.rationale: With the physical infrastructure understood, you can now master how people work inside it: garbing, technique, critical site protection, and how the monitoring programs verify that the environment and personnel remain in compliance.

phases.label: Advanced — Sterilization Science and Hazardous Drugs
phases.rationale: Sterilization methods and BET testing build on everything prior — you need to understand what sterility means before you learn how to verify it. Hazardous drug content is last because it overlays an additional safety layer on top of all preceding concepts.

studyPath.studyTip: After Phase 1, memorize the BUD table until you can recall all limits without looking. BUD category logic is the most frequently tested concept across every USP 797 competency assessment, accreditation survey, and pharmacist interview in sterile compounding.

examPrep.title: High-Yield Exam and Accreditation Survey Topics

items: SCA vs cleanroom suite → which category of BUD is achievable
Category 1: 12h room temp / 24h refrigerated — no frozen
Category 2 aqueous without sterility testing: 45h / 60h / 365h (RT/refrig/frozen)
Category 2 aqueous with sterility testing: 90d / 120d / 365d
Sterility testing extends Category 2 BUD ONLY — not Category 1
BUD assigned at time of compounding — cannot be extended retroactively
Initial qualification = 3 separate passing GFS AND 3 separate passing media fills
GFS action level ≤1 CFU per gloved hand at ISO 5
Ongoing qualification frequency: every 6 months
Media fill failure = immediate suspension, 3 consecutive re-qualifications to return
Who may observe garbing: trained assessor — cannot self-assess
LAF (positive pressure) = never for HDs
BSC Class II = for hazardous drug compounding
Certification frequency: every 6 months
ISO 5 inside ISO 7 = Category 2 possible
ISO 5 in unclassified space (SCA) = Category 1 only
ACPH requirement: ≥30 total, ≥15 from HEPA in ISO 7
Cleaning always before disinfection — organic matter neutralizes disinfectant
Sterile 70% IPA required in ISO 5 and ISO 7 — non-sterile IPA is NOT compliant
Sporicidal agent: minimum monthly, documented, rotated
Dwell time must be observed — most commonly skipped step
Cleaning direction: cleanest to most contaminated, top to bottom
Deactivation before decontamination — order is not interchangeable
C-PEC must be negative pressure — no exceptions
NIOSH list Group 1 = antineoplastics (highest occupational risk)
USP 800 training required for ALL HD handlers (not just pharmacy)
Double chemo-rated gloves (ASTM D6978), chemo gown (AAMI PB70 Level 2 minimum)
C-SEC: -0.01 to -0.03 inches WC negative pressure
0.22 µm filter removes bacteria — does NOT remove endotoxins
Autoclave: sterilizes but does NOT depyrogenate
Dry heat 250°C = depyrogenation (endotoxin destruction)
Intrathecal endotoxin limit = 0.2 EU/kg/hr (most stringent route)
Sterility testing: 14-day incubation, two temperature ranges

mnemonics.mnemonic: Shoes → Hair → Face → Wash → Gown → Gloves
mnemonics.detail: Shoe covers, hair/beard cover, face mask, 30-sec hand/forearm wash, gown, THEN sterile gloves last

mnemonics.mnemonic: 3 D's in order: Deactivate → Decontaminate → Disinfect
mnemonics.detail: Deactivate (chemical neutralization) → Decontaminate (physical removal) → Disinfect (microbial reduction)
SCA gives Category 1 (12h/24h). Full cleanroom suite gives Category 2 (45d/60d/365d without sterility test)

mnemonics.mnemonic: Filter stops bugs, not toxins — heat kills both
mnemonics.detail: 0.22µm filter removes organisms; endotoxins pass through. Dry heat at 250°C destroys both organisms and endotoxins

mnemonics.mnemonic: 5 = Spot (direct work surface), 7 = Suite (buffer room), 8 = Staging (anteroom)
mnemonics.detail: ISO 5 = direct compounding surface (PEC), ISO 7 = buffer room housing PEC, ISO 8 = anteroom/garbing

mnemonics.mnemonic: STOP-FIND-FIX-CHECK: Stop compounding, Find root cause, Fix it (CAPA), Check (re-sample)
mnemonics.detail: Action-level excursion requires: halt compounding, root cause investigation, documented CAPA with timeline, verification re-sampling

commonSurveyorFindings: Non-sterile IPA used inside ISO-classified areas (sterile IPA required)
Sporicidal agent rotation not documented or schedule not written
Personnel qualification records incomplete or not immediately retrievable
Category 2 BUDs assigned to CSPs prepared in an SCA
SOPs not reviewed within 2-year cycle or not accessible at point of use
Garbing sequence deviation observed but not documented or corrected
PEC certification lapsed or CO credentials not verified
Endotoxin testing not performed for Category 2 aqueous CSPs
USP 800 training records absent for non-pharmacy staff handling HDs
EM program lacks defined sample map with pre-specified locations
